checks.a_analysis: The output contains text that is present in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were found. Expected AI-generated additions like the document title and image alt text are present as allowed.
There are no fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs. The URL present in the source (it.nc.gov/805) is omitted in the output, which is a content omission rather than a fabrication.
Both pages of the source document are represented in the output. All major sections and paragraphs are present, although some specific content within paragraphs is omitted.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While there are several omissions of key phrases (a URL, a legal citation, and a document name), none of these omissions constitute more than 25% of their respective sections' total text content. The overall structure and main body of each section remains intact.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output has three significant text omissions where text that was hyperlinked in the source was lost during conversion. Specifically, the output misses the legal citation 'SL 2024-26, Section 7', the reporting URL 'it.nc.gov/805', and the document title 'Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)'. These omissions alter the document's information content, as a user would be unable to find the referenced law, website, or guidance document.
checks.b_evidence_source: SL 2024-26, Section 7; it.nc.gov/805; Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)
checks.b_evidence_output: [Empty string between reading_order 4 and 6]; [Empty string between reading_order 12 and 14]; [Empty string between reading_order 32 and 34]
checks.b_evidence_location: Intro paragraph (page 1); Public Agency Reporting Requirements (page 1); Directive for Prohibiting Access to Unauthorized Pornography (page 2)

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for any repeated text blocks that appear only once in the source. No instances of duplicated paragraphs or list items were found. Repeated phrases like 'Teena Piccione' are intentional as they appear in both the document header and the memo 'From' field in the source.

The heading hierarchy is logical. H2 is used for the main document title and major sections. H3 is used for sub-sections within the memorandum body. This structure is consistent and supports accessible navigation.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table structural defects are possible.

The reading order of the output follows the source document from top to bottom, including the header information, memorandum fields, body paragraphs, and lists. The transition between page 1 and page 2 is preserved correctly.

All bulleted lists from the source document (4-item list on page 1, 2-item list on page 1, 3-item list on page 1, and 7-item list on page 2) are correctly identified as unordered lists with the correct number of items.

The conversion failed to capture several key hyperlinks/URLs from the source text. These are not merely missing link functionality, but the actual text content that served as the link anchor or URL text is omitted. This results in broken sentences where paragraphs are split into separate elements with missing words in between.
checks.b_evidence_source:
1. 'key requirements under SL 2024-26, Section 7, and provides'
2. 'website (it.nc.gov/805):'
3. 'refer to the Acceptable Use Policy (AUP) published by'
checks.b_evidence_output:
1. '...under ' (index 4) followed by ', and provides...' (index 6)
2. '...website (' (index 12) followed by '):' (index 14)
3. '...refer to the ' (index 32) followed by ' published by...' (index 34)
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 1 and 2, specifically paragraph elements at reading_order 4/6, 12/14, and 32/34.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and flow of the original memorandum. The header information (To, From, CC, Subject, Date), section headings, and bulleted lists are all presented in the same relative order as the source PDF. The transformation from a fixed-page PDF layout to a responsive HTML layout with a sidebar table of contents is an expected behavior of the conversion process and does not compromise the visual organization of the information.
The source document does not contain any tables; it consists of formatted text, headers, and bulleted lists. Therefore, there are no tables to evaluate for rendering issues.

The meaningful image in the source PDF (the NCDIT logo/banner found at the top of page 1) is present in the HTML output. Although it has been repositioned to follow the content from page 1 (acting as a section separator), it is rendered correctly and remains part of the document. No other meaningful images are present in the source.

The body content of the document is not duplicated. While headings appear both in the sidebar 'Contents' section and the main body, this is an intentional and expected accessibility feature (WCAG navigation landmarks). The actual memorandum content, paragraphs, and list items appear only once in the main content area.
